data.title: Huaneng Power Revamps Company Secretary Team After Retirement of Veteran Executive
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Huaneng Power International ( (HK:0902) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Huaneng Power International announced that long-serving company secretary and authorised representative Huang Chaoquan has retired, with the board noting there were no disagreements and expressing appreciation for his contributions. The company has appointed chief accountant and board secretary-designate Wen Minggang as joint company secretary and authorised representative, alongside external legal expert Jiang Xiao, whose background in cross-border M&A and corporate finance is expected to strengthen the firm’s governance and capital markets interface.

The appointments, effective 24 March 2026, come as Huaneng Power aligns its leadership structure with Hong Kong Listing Rules while obtaining a waiver to facilitate compliance with professional qualification requirements for company secretaries. The addition of a senior internal finance executive and an experienced international lawyer to the joint company secretary roles underscores the utility’s efforts to reinforce regulatory oversight, investor communication, and board support amid an increasingly complex capital markets and energy sector environment.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0902) stock is a Hold with a HK$5.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Huaneng Power International stock, see the HK:0902 Stock Forecast page.

More about Huaneng Power International

Huaneng Power International, Inc. is a major Chinese power producer focused on the generation and sale of electricity, with listings in multiple markets including Hong Kong. The company operates within China’s state-linked energy ecosystem and plays a significant role in conventional and renewable power supply to industrial, commercial, and residential customers.

Average Trading Volume: 36,086,532

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$118.9B

data.title: Huaneng Power Takes RMB1.5 Billion Profit Hit on 2025 Asset Impairments
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Huaneng Power International ( (HK:0902) ) has issued an announcement.

Huaneng Power International has conducted an impairment review for 2025, leading to substantial write-downs on several generating assets and receivables in the fourth quarter. The company recognized consolidated asset impairment provisions of RMB1.396 billion and credit impairment provisions of RMB105 million, mainly linked to plant shutdown or standby policies, environmental and other regulatory requirements, sustained operating losses at certain new energy and gas projects, and specific unusable or dismantled equipment.

These impairments reduced the group’s consolidated total profit by RMB1.501 billion and the parent company’s profit by RMB52 million for the period, signaling the financial impact of policy-driven capacity adjustments and underperforming assets. The move reflects management’s attempt to present a more accurate financial position, while highlighting ongoing operational and regulatory pressures on conventional and selected new energy facilities within China’s power sector transition.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0902) stock is a Hold with a HK$5.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Huaneng Power International stock, see the HK:0902 Stock Forecast page.

More about Huaneng Power International

Huaneng Power International, Inc. is a major Chinese power generation company, operating a portfolio of coal-fired, gas-fired, hydropower, and new energy plants. The company focuses on supplying electricity and heat across multiple provinces, and its assets are subject to evolving environmental and energy policy requirements in mainland China and Hong Kong capital markets.

Average Trading Volume: 36,086,532

Jiangsu Recbio Technology Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2179) ) just unveiled an update.

Jiangsu Recbio Technology announced a reshuffle of key governance roles, with the resignation of company secretary, authorised representative and Hong Kong process agent Ms. Yung Mei Yee, effective 24 March 2026. The board noted that Ms. Yung has no disagreements with directors and no issues requiring shareholder or exchange attention, signalling a routine transition rather than a dispute-driven departure.

The company simultaneously appointed executive director and board secretary Mr. Wei Qifang and corporate governance specialist Ms. Suen Ka Yan as joint company secretaries, with Wei also becoming authorised representative and Suen serving as Hong Kong process agent. Wei brings three decades of experience across finance, investment banking, risk control and corporate governance, while Suen adds specialised Hong Kong corporate secretarial expertise, moves that appear aimed at strengthening Recbio’s compliance infrastructure and investor-facing capabilities as it navigates capital markets and regulatory demands.

data.title: Huaneng Power International Announces Proposed 2025 Final Dividend and Tax Terms for H Shareholders
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An announcement from Huaneng Power International ( (HK:0902) ) is now available.

Huaneng Power International has proposed a final dividend of RMB 0.4 per share for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, with key timetable details such as shareholder approval date, ex-dividend date, record date, and payment date to be announced. The company has also outlined the applicable withholding tax arrangements for non-resident individual and enterprise H shareholders, specifying different tax rates based on residency and tax treaties with the PRC, which will directly affect the net dividend income of overseas investors.

Individual non-resident H shareholders generally face a 10% withholding tax, rising to 20% if their home countries lack favorable tax treaties with the PRC or set higher treaty rates. Non-resident enterprise shareholders, including those whose H shares are registered under nominee names such as HKSCC Nominees Limited, will be subject to a 10% enterprise income tax on the 2025 final dividend, clarifying post-tax returns for institutional investors and other corporate holders.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0902) stock is a Hold with a HK$5.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Huaneng Power International stock, see the HK:0902 Stock Forecast page.

More about Huaneng Power International

Huaneng Power International, Inc. is a major power generation company, focusing on the production and sale of electricity. The company primarily serves the Chinese market, including investors holding H shares listed in Hong Kong, and is subject to mainland Chinese tax regulations on dividends paid to non-resident shareholders.

OneRobotics (Shenzhen) Co., Ltd. plans to adopt a 2026 H Share Incentive Scheme using existing H shares to attract, motivate and retain key R&D and management personnel, particularly those contributing to its long-term and sustainable development. Nanfang Communication Holdings has warned that its profit and total comprehensive income for the year ended 31 December 2025 is expected to fall to between RMB28.0 million and RMB31.0 million, down from about RMB39.9 million a year earlier. data.title: Xuanzhu’s Breast Cancer Drug Bireociclib Delivers Strong Phase 3 Results Published in JAMA Oncology
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Xuanzhu Biopharmaceutical Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2575) ) just unveiled an update.

Xuanzhu Biopharmaceutical said final Phase 3 data from its BRIGHT-2 trial of bireociclib plus fulvestrant in HR-positive, HER2-negative advanced breast cancer have been published in leading journal JAMA Oncology. The multicenter, randomized study in China evaluated patients whose disease had progressed after prior endocrine therapy, extending median follow-up to 19 months to confirm durability of benefit.

The combination markedly improved median progression-free survival versus placebo, cutting the risk of disease progression or death by about half and showing a median PFS of up to 17.5 months under blinded central review. The regimen also delivered a substantially higher objective response rate, durable tumor shrinkage across multiple cycles, and consistent benefit in high-risk clinical and molecular subgroups, underscoring bireociclib’s competitive efficacy and safety profile in a crowded CDK inhibitor class.

Investigators highlighted that bireociclib’s design as a highly selective CDK4 inhibitor with weaker CDK6 activity may help mitigate hematologic toxicity while supporting continuous twice-daily dosing to maintain stable target inhibition. These data strengthen Xuanzhu’s position in the breast cancer treatment landscape and could enhance the commercial and clinical prospects of bireociclib as a differentiated option for patients with advanced HR-positive, HER2-negative disease.

More about Xuanzhu Biopharmaceutical Co., Ltd. Class H

Xuanzhu Biopharmaceutical Co., Ltd. is a China-based biopharmaceutical company focused on developing innovative oncology therapies. Nongfu Spring Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:9633) ) has issued an update.

Nongfu Spring Co., Ltd., a major Chinese beverage and bottled water company listed in Hong Kong, reported robust growth for the year ended 31 December 2025. The group continues to build on its strong market position in China’s fast-growing packaged drinks sector, supported by broad product coverage and nationwide distribution.

data.title: Fujian Haixi Advances First-in-Class Oral Retinal Drug HXP056 Into Phase II
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An announcement from Fujian Haixi Pharmaceuticals Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2637) ) is now available.

Fujian Haixi Pharmaceuticals has reported key progress on HXP056, an oral small-molecule candidate that aims to be the world’s first pill-based therapy for hemorrhage-related retinal diseases, including wet age-related macular degeneration, diabetic macular edema and retinal vein occlusion. Built on the firm’s MultiSel-Opt Platform, HXP056 is engineered to cross the blood-retinal barrier while limiting systemic exposure, positioning the programme as both a technological milestone and a potentially large global market opportunity.

The company has completed patient enrollment for both single and multiple ascending dose cohorts in a Phase I trial for wet age-related macular degeneration in China and has gathered dose-limiting toxicity, pharmacokinetic and preliminary efficacy data. Early results indicate favourable safety, tolerability and initial improvements in retinal structure and function, enabling Haixi to start a Phase II dose-expansion study in late 2025 as it works to identify an optimal dose and prepare for Phase III, though the company cautions that regulatory approval and commercialisation remain subject to lengthy, uncertain clinical and review processes.

More about Fujian Haixi Pharmaceuticals Co., Ltd. Class H

Fujian Haixi Pharmaceuticals Co., Ltd. is a China-based biopharmaceutical company focused on innovative small-molecule therapies in inflammation and immune regulation. Leveraging its proprietary MultiSel-Opt Platform, the company designs multi-selective oral drug candidates targeting complex diseases, with particular emphasis on penetrating barriers such as the blood-retinal and blood-brain barriers for ophthalmic and neurological indications.

The latest update is out from China Telecom ( (HK:0728) ).

China Telecom has proposed a final ordinary dividend of RMB 0.0908 per share for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, with shareholder approval scheduled for 19 May 2026 and payment set for 8 July 2026. Details including the Hong Kong dollar equivalent, exchange rate, ex-dividend date, record date, and withholding tax arrangements will be announced later, leaving investors awaiting clarity on the effective yield and tax treatment of the payout.
